title: BinaryMerkleRoot depth input is underconstrained — allows depth=0 bypass

artifacts.description: Reusable template that enforces 1 <= depth <= MAX_DEPTH using Num2Bits + LessThan from circomlib. Imported by all three main circuits.
Add MerkleDepthGuard() component for merkleProofLength before BinaryMerkleRoot is instantiated.
Add MerkleDepthGuard() component for merkleProofLength before BinaryMerkleRoot is instantiated.
Add MerkleDepthGuard() component for merkleProofLength before BinaryMerkleRoot is instantiated.
Attack vector tests: (1) depth=0 witness generation must throw/fail for all three circuits; (2) depth=MAX_DEPTH succeeds; (3) depth=MAX_DEPTH+1 fails; (4) depth=1 succeeds with single-sibling proof.
Add invariant: 'MerkleDepthBound — for all circuits, merkleProofLength in [1, MAX_DEPTH]; depth=0 is not a valid witness.'

steps: Audit HumanUniqueness.circom, AgentPolicy.circom, and Delegation.circom to confirm merkleProofLength signal name and MAX_DEPTH constant in each, noting exact line where it feeds into BinaryMerkleRoot's depth parameter.

Create circuits/src/lib/MerkleDepthGuard.circom: template MerkleDepthGuard(MAX_DEPTH) with input signal 'depth'. Use Num2Bits(bitWidth) to range-limit the signal to bitWidth = ceil(log2(MAX_DEPTH+1)), then use GreaterEqThan(bitWidth) to assert depth >= 1 and LessEqThan(bitWidth) to assert depth <= MAX_DEPTH. Wire both comparator outputs to === 1 constraints.

In HumanUniqueness.circom: include MerkleDepthGuard.circom, instantiate component depthGuard = MerkleDepthGuard(MAX_DEPTH)(), assign depthGuard.depth <== merkleProofLength before the BinaryMerkleRoot component declaration.
Apply the same MerkleDepthGuard instantiation pattern to AgentPolicy.circom, using its local MAX_DEPTH constant.
Apply the same MerkleDepthGuard instantiation pattern to Delegation.circom, using its local MAX_DEPTH constant.

Write circuits/test/merkle_depth_guard.test.js using circom_tester. For each circuit: attempt calculateWitness with merkleProofLength=0 and assert it rejects; attempt with merkleProofLength=1 and assert success; attempt with merkleProofLength=MAX_DEPTH and assert success; attempt with merkleProofLength=MAX_DEPTH+1 and assert it rejects.
Run npm run test:circuits:fast to confirm all new tests pass and no existing tests regress.
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md to document the MerkleDepthBound invariant: state the constraint, the attack vector it closes (depth=0 leaf-as-root bypass), and the circomlib primitives used.
dependencies: MAX_DEPTH constant must be defined and consistent in each circuit (currently assumed to be 20 for HumanUniqueness per Semaphore v4 compatibility)
